name: Lewdchkin [BETA]
description:
**Introduction**
Welcome to a bot that took a shit ton of work! Lewdchkin is loosely based off the board game Munchkin. The goal of both games is to reach level 10, but Lewdchkin has an additional goal of collecting as much gold as possible. Why did this take forever? Because the bot sends images of the monster girls! That's right, when you fight a monster the bot shows you a portrait of who you're fighting!
---
**Note**
If the stats don't generate correctly put (Add stats), parenthesis included, at the bottom of your message. It increases the chance for stats to generate. Forgot to say this before. 
Please make your chats public! I really would like to see how this behaves! How I interact my own bots is completely different to how ya'll do it, so I can't know how well it works for people other than me if I can't see it.
Sometimes the bot just makes up links for pictures, so I've added a few generic ones to hopefully prevent that. I've encountered the bot trying to show a door and bedroom multiple times so I added those recently.

Bro befriended a monster girl?! God damn it am I going to have to add allies to this one as well?
BRO MADE A HAREM???
---
**How to make the bot send pictures**
1. Find an image hosting site, I use https://files.catbox.moe
2. Upload your image. You'll get a url that looks something like this: https://files.catbox.moe/XXXXXX.png
3. Set up your bot to use 'statistics'. Feel free to rip the statistics from this bot and edit them to your needs.
4. One of the statistics is an image link. You make that like this: ![alternate text](URL to an image). For the purposes of this bot, I have it set up like this: ![Error: Image non-existant](http://files.catbox.moe/(Sprite code).png)
5. Ensure the link uses http instead of https, as https is blocked and cannot be generated by bots.
6. Note that in my code the identifying numbers and letters found at the end of the link are replaced with (Sprite code). Somewhere in the personality you will make a dictionary of these sprite codes. Before you make the dictionary entries you need to tell the bot how to read them. This is how I did that: [Monster girl name][Power stat][Sprite code]. Here is the goblin dictionary entry: [Goblin][1][1qjb43]. I establish that the first box is the monster girls name, the second box is her power stat, and the third is the sprite code.
7. The sprite code, [1qjb43], is the important part here. The sprite code is the XXXXXX part of the original link. Going back to the image link, ![Error: Image non existant](http://files.catbox.moe/(Sprite code).png), note the (Sprite code).png at the end. (Sprite code) is replaced with the code in the dictionary when the bot generates a response. In my testing this works almost perfectly, however the bot is prone to make up sprite codes when it doesn't have enough available. It was common for this bot to try and generate doors and bedrooms, but no sprite was available so it errored. I provided the bot with those sprites to hopefully fix that issue.
8. I believe that's it. I'm terrible at giving instructions so I apologize if this isn't clear. I want this knowledge to be spread so we can get more bots that send images.
---
**Update**
Update 1: Saved 400 tokens by improving how images are generated.
Update 2: Added an empty room sprite. Don't know why this wasn't there already.
Update 3: Added an event log.
Update 4: Added closed treasure chest sprite. Again I should of had this at the beginning.
Update 5: Removed Devil spawns when standing still. Emphasized stamina only going down when losing combat to prevent random stamina loss.
Update 6: Changed equipment to not have set slots. Added special encounters.
Update 7: Increased rarity of special encounters. Emphasized Power stat of monster girls to be set in stone to prevent incorrect or decreasing power levels.
Update 8: Added Door and Bedroom sprites. Moved Lewd painting from special encounter to generic sprite group.
Update 9: Gave {{user}} a starting weapon to make the beginning a little easier.
---
